reasoning: While composite scores are exceptionally high (0.9889) and programmatic metrics show excellent content preservation, a specific discrepancy was flagged regarding semantic link tagging. The failure to convert a visible URL ('www.nconemap.net') on page 7 into a functional link object, combined with a significant mismatch between JSON and HTML link counts, suggests a minor but inconsistent application of accessibility standards that requires human verification.

concerns.analysis: A URL clearly visible in the source text on page 7 ('www.nconemap.net') is not converted into a clickable link in the JSON output; it is rendered only as underlined text. In contrast, URLs on page 2 ('www.ncstreams.net') and page 10 ('www.ncgicc.org/') are correctly converted into link objects with href attributes. The link count mismatch in metadata (JSON: 2, HTML: 12) suggests the renderer added navigational links (skip links, etc.), but the missing functional link in the JSON content is a min
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The map of the initial 23 counties that responded to the preliminary inquiry is on the NC OneMap website, www.nconemap.net. (Page 7)
concerns.location: Section 1, paragraph beginning 'Zsolt Nagy mentioned that a third year...'

Investigate the discrepancy between the 2 links identified in JSON and the 12 links reported in HTML metadata to ensure no content links were missed.
